name: Blackwell risk monotonicity
target: Under common state/action/loss spaces and unrestricted measurable decision rules, a Blackwell-more-informative experiment cannot have larger optimal risk.
c85t_obligation: state measurable-space and randomization assumptions and provide a self-contained proof or exact cited theorem mapping

name: Restricted-policy nonmonotonicity
target: A finite Blackwell-dominating experiment can have higher realized registered-policy risk when policy classes are non-nested or the richer policy is poorly optimized.
c85t_obligation: exhaustively enumerate the protocol-locked finite example

name: Policy-collapse equivalence
target: Almost-sure equality of two fixed action rules implies identical action loss and risk and zero incremental realized action value, without implying experiment equivalence.

name: Two-state Le Cam regret lower bound
target: For equal-prior two-state finite models with distinct optimal actions and wrong-action regret at least Delta, prove the exact average-risk lower bound in terms of total variation.
c85t_obligation: derive constants, handle randomization and state the action-separation condition; do not assume candidate form

name: Finite multi-candidate Fano extension
target: A finite packing lower bound for candidate-selection regret under separated optimal actions.
c85t_obligation: define packing, information bound, loss separation and exact constants; may remain OPEN

name: Mean-tail non-equivalence
target: A finite policy comparison with positive mean improvement and majority benefit but worse worst-group and upper-tail CVaR risk.
c85t_obligation: exhaustively verify the protocol-locked group-loss example for symbolic alpha intervals

name: Near-optimal-set selection bound
target: Under explicitly registered sub-Gaussian pairwise utility-estimation errors, bound selection outside A_epsilon using gap- and scale-weighted terms rather than raw M.
c85t_obligation: prove dependence structure or use a valid union bound and identify every constant
